C. elegans Smaller Gene Set GO Analysis - Manuscript Method

clusterProfiler over-representation analysis with BH correction

June 01, 2026

  • Purpose
  • Packages
  • Gene Input
  • ID Mapping
  • Manuscript-Style GO Enrichment
    • Biological Process
    • Molecular Function
    • Cellular Component
  • Export Results
  • Conclusion

Purpose

This report reruns the smaller C. elegans gene set using the same over-representation approach used in the manuscript-style analysis: clusterProfiler::enrichGO() with Benjamini-Hochberg (BH) multiple-testing correction. This is intentionally different from the later topGO analysis. The goal is to document that the smaller gene set does not yield significant BH-adjusted GO terms under the stricter manuscript method.

Packages

required_pkgs <- c(
  "readxl", "dplyr", "ggplot2", "clusterProfiler", "org.Ce.eg.db",
  "enrichplot", "knitr", "kableExtra"
)

missing_pkgs <- required_pkgs[
  !sapply(required_pkgs, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)
]
if (length(missing_pkgs) > 0) {
  BiocManager::install(missing_pkgs, ask = FALSE)
}

invisible(lapply(required_pkgs, library, character.only = TRUE))

Gene Input

file_path_worm <- "./05-2026/list of genes_resubmission.xlsx"
worm_df <- readxl::read_excel(file_path_worm)

wb_col <- grep("WormBase.*Gene|WBGene", names(worm_df),
               ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)[1]
sym_col <- grep("^Gene$|Gene.*[Ss]ymbol|[Ss]ymbol|gene.*name",
                names(worm_df), ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE)[1]

cat("Input file:", file_path_worm, "\n")
## Input file: ./05-2026/list of genes_resubmission.xlsx
cat("Total input genes:", nrow(worm_df), "\n")
## Total input genes: 18
cat("WormBase ID column:", wb_col, "\n")
## WormBase ID column: WormBase Gene ID
cat("Gene symbol column:", sym_col, "\n")
## Gene symbol column: Gene

ID Mapping

map_to_entrez <- function(ids, from_type) {
  ids <- na.omit(unique(ids))
  if (length(ids) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(ENTREZID = character()))
  }

  suppressMessages(
    clusterProfiler::bitr(
      ids,
      fromType = from_type,
      toType = "ENTREZID",
      OrgDb = org.Ce.eg.db
    )
  )
}

wb_ids <- if (!is.na(wb_col)) na.omit(unique(worm_df[[wb_col]])) else character()
sym_ids <- if (!is.na(sym_col)) na.omit(unique(worm_df[[sym_col]])) else character()

map_wb <- map_to_entrez(wb_ids, "WORMBASE")
map_sym <- map_to_entrez(sym_ids, "SYMBOL")

mapping_summary <- data.frame(
  `ID type` = c("WormBase (WORMBASE)", "Gene Symbol (SYMBOL)"),
  `Input genes` = c(length(wb_ids), length(sym_ids)),
  `Mapped Entrez IDs` = c(
    length(unique(map_wb$ENTREZID)),
    length(unique(map_sym$ENTREZID))
  ),
  check.names = FALSE
)

knitr::kable(
  mapping_summary,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption = "Entrez ID mapping summary for the smaller gene set"
) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped"),
    font_size = 10
  )
Entrez ID mapping summary for the smaller gene set
ID type Input genes Mapped Entrez IDs
WormBase (WORMBASE) 18 18
Gene Symbol (SYMBOL) 18 18 
if (length(unique(map_sym$ENTREZID)) > length(unique(map_wb$ENTREZID))) {
  worm_entrez <- unique(map_sym$ENTREZID)
  id_method <- "Gene Symbol -> Entrez"
} else {
  worm_entrez <- unique(map_wb$ENTREZID)
  id_method <- "WormBase ID -> Entrez"
}

cat("Selected mapping method:", id_method, "\n")
## Selected mapping method: WormBase ID -> Entrez
cat("Mapped query genes:", length(worm_entrez), "\n")
## Mapped query genes: 18

Manuscript-Style GO Enrichment

GO over-representation analysis was performed using enrichGO() with BH multiple-testing correction. All terms were retained for reporting and plotting, but terms were considered significant only if they met the adjusted threshold used in the manuscript-style workflow.

run_go <- function(ontology) {
  clusterProfiler::enrichGO(
    gene = worm_entrez,
    OrgDb = org.Ce.eg.db,
    keyType = "ENTREZID",
    ont = ontology,
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff = 1,
    qvalueCutoff = 1,
    minGSSize = 3,
    readable = TRUE
  )
}

ego_ce_bp <- run_go("BP")
ego_ce_mf <- run_go("MF")
ego_ce_cc <- run_go("CC")

bp_all <- ego_ce_bp@result
mf_all <- ego_ce_mf@result
cc_all <- ego_ce_cc@result

result_summary <- data.frame(
  Ontology = c("Biological Process", "Molecular Function", "Cellular Component"),
  `Terms tested/returned` = c(nrow(bp_all), nrow(mf_all), nrow(cc_all)),
  `Nominal raw p < 0.05` = c(
    sum(bp_all$pvalue < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(mf_all$pvalue < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(cc_all$pvalue < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  `Terms with BH adjusted p < 0.05` = c(
    sum(bp_all$p.adjust < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(mf_all$p.adjust < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(cc_all$p.adjust < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  `Terms with q < 0.2` = c(
    sum(bp_all$qvalue < 0.2, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(mf_all$qvalue < 0.2, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(cc_all$qvalue < 0.2, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  check.names = FALSE
)

knitr::kable(
  result_summary,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption = "Summary of manuscript-style GO enrichment results"
) |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped"),
    font_size = 10
  )
Summary of manuscript-style GO enrichment results
Ontology Terms tested/returned Nominal raw p < 0.05 Terms with BH adjusted p < 0.05 Terms with q < 0.2
Biological Process 3683 102 0 0
Molecular Function 1299 25 0 0
Cellular Component 758 13 0 0

Biological Process

if (nrow(bp_all) == 0) {
  cat("No Biological Process GO terms were significant after BH correction.")
} else {
  bp_show <- bp_all |>
    dplyr::arrange(pvalue) |>
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 20) |>
    dplyr::select(
      ID, Description, GeneRatio, BgRatio, pvalue, p.adjust, qvalue, geneID, Count
    )

  knitr::kable(
    bp_show,
    booktabs = TRUE,
    digits = 4,
    caption = "C. elegans Biological Process GO results using manuscript method"
  ) |>
    kableExtra::kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("scale_down", "hold_position", "striped"),
      font_size = 8
    )
}

Export Results

out_dir <- "output_tables_smaller_gene_set_manuscript_method"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

write.csv(bp_all, file.path(out_dir, "Worm_GO_BP_BH_corrected.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(mf_all, file.path(out_dir, "Worm_GO_MF_BH_corrected.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(cc_all, file.path(out_dir, "Worm_GO_CC_BH_corrected.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(result_summary, file.path(out_dir, "GO_BH_corrected_summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

cat("Results exported to", out_dir, "\n")
## Results exported to output_tables_smaller_gene_set_manuscript_method

Conclusion

Using the manuscript-style clusterProfiler::enrichGO() workflow with BH correction, the smaller C. elegans query set did not produce significant GO enrichment terms. This supports the interpretation that the lack of significance is due to the small query size and multiple-testing burden, rather than a failure of ID mapping. The later topGO weight01 analysis should therefore be viewed as a hierarchy-aware follow-up method for extracting directional biological signal from a small, focused gene list.
